design_philosophy.core_loop: Progress through Neverland levels, defeat enemies with melee/ranged weapons, collect health/power-ups, reach Captain Hook boss fight
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Resource scarcity (limited health leaves, limited flight duration with pixie dust) versus enemy encounters and platforming hazards
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as Peter Pan rescuing children from Captain Hook, experiencing Neverland's diverse locations with signature mechanics (flying, sword combat)
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Film license adaptation with explicit narrative framing (rescue mission); health system tied to thematic leaf mechanic; flight as limited resource rather than unlimited mobility; weapon degradation forces tactical choices

difficulty.lives_system: Health meter measured in leaves (starts with 2, maximum 4 collectible); losing all leaves triggers failure state
difficulty.checkpointing: No password/save feature documented; level-based progression structure implies per-level checkpoints at minimum
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Health management through fruit collection; flight duration management via pixie dust collection; weapon choice (dagger vs. golden sword tradeoff)

progression.upgrade_system: Weapon progression: dagger → golden sword (with energy ball projectiles) after level 1; health leaf collection; pixie dust accumulation for flight ability
progression.unlock_mechanism: Golden sword unlocked after completing first level; flight ability granted via pixie dust item pickups; subsequent levels unlocked sequentially
replayability_hooks: Inability to save/password encourages speedrun approach
Varied level environments (caves, forests, lagoons, snowy mountains) create distinct challenges

world_building_techniques: Diverse Neverland environments reflect source material geography
Enemy variety tied to thematic setting (pirates, spiders, snakes, skeletons)
Digitized film sequences in Sega CD version enhance cinematic narrative
Captain Hook's ship introduction sequence establishes final encounter stakes

boss_design.philosophy: Climactic final confrontation matching film narrative; sword duel between Peter Pan and Captain Hook
notable_bosses: Captain Hook (final level) - sword fight conclusion to rescue mission narrative

faction_indicators: Lost Boys (secondary playable options in arcade version)
danger_signals: Environmental hazards in Neverland (caves, snowy mountains suggest treacherous terrain)
Health leaf scarcity creates persistent vulnerability state
Weapon loss upon damage increases risk exposure
environmental_storytelling: Pirate Town location establishes Hook's territorial control
Diverse biome progression (forests, lagoons, caves, mountains) depicts expansive Neverland geography
Pixie dust distribution suggests Tinker Bell's presence guiding Peter Pan
Enemy placement by location type reinforces environmental character
Golden sword appearance after first level marks power progression milestone
